deck: Part two of Dimagi's founding story: the shift to open source, the launch of CommCare as a SaaS product, the early hires who shaped the company, and how a scrappy group of friends grew into a global organization over 20 years.
seo_description: Part two of Dimagi's founding story with Jonathan Jackson and Dr. Neal Lesh: open source, the CommCare SaaS pivot, key hires, and 20 years of cultural change.
og_description: Dimagi's founding story, part two: open source, the CommCare SaaS pivot, key hires, and how the culture grew up.

pull_quote.text: We understood that we were those mice and we were trying to take over the world. And that was a pretty distinctive framing and attitude and ambition relative to the other groups.
pull_quote.cite: Dr. Neal Lesh, on Dimagi's early culture

bullets: The sustainability problem: why software impact comes from running a system for years, not from reaching deployment, and how that reframed the business model
From open source to SaaS: contributing to platforms like OpenMRS and RapidSMS, then offering CommCare as a low recurring fee to give projects a path to sustainability
Engineering for the last mile: how Clayton Sims and Corey Zoo set a culture willing to sacrifice the ideal engineering problem to reach the hardest-to-reach 20 percent of users
Aligned from the start: how the early partnership with D-Tree and Neal Lesh, focused on the program rather than donor reports, shaped CommCare
The PEPFAR wave: how a surge of US government AIDS funding after 2005 reshaped the digital health landscape Dimagi grew up in
Growing up: moving from Jon's dorm room and sprint-mode hustle toward a diverse global team, strong people operations, and a marathon mentality
Impact, team, profit: the core ordering Jon says has never changed as the company has scaled

stats.label: of Dimagi's evolution in mobile and digital health covered in this founding story
of Dimagi's revenue at the time tied to a project the company was willing to walk away from to stay on its impact path
the year Neal Lesh sees as the start of the current era of digital development, boosted by PEPFAR funding

guests.name: Dr. Neal Lesh
guests.role: Chief Strategy Officer and one of Dimagi's critical early hires, an early collaborator with Jonathan Jackson through D-Tree and the formation of CommCare.
